title: unnecessary %Warning-UNSIGNED: Comparison is constant due to unsigned arithmetic ?
body:
---


Author Name: **Alex Solomatnikov**
Original Redmine Issue: 456 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2012-03-12
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

The following test case:

```
typedef logic signed [35-1:0] rc_t;

module test_negative( output logic o,
                       input rc_t   rc,
                       input logic  clk);

    localparam RATIO = 2;

    rc_t rc_d[RATIO:1];

    always_ff @(posedge clk) begin
       integer  k;

       rc_d[1] <= rc;
       
       for( k=2; k<RATIO+1; k++ ) begin
          rc_d[k] <= rc_d[k-1];
       end
    end // always_ff @

    assign o = rc_d[RATIO] < 0;

endmodule // test_negative

```

causes warning:

```
verilator -sv --sp --top-module test_negative test_negative.v
%Warning-UNSIGNED: test_negative.v:21: Comparison is constant due to unsigned arithmetic
%Warning-UNSIGNED: Use "/* verilator lint_off UNSIGNED */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.

```

However, simplified version:

```
typedef logic signed [35-1:0] rc_t;

module test_negative( output logic o,
                       input rc_t   rc,
                       input logic  clk);

    assign o = rc < 0;

endmodule // test_negative

```

does not cause warning.
